sentence_reviews.text: Ordered observations represent language, events, trajectories, biological sequences, and temporal processes.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: PML book1 long TOC: neural networks for sequences, sequence generation/classification/translation, language models, and time-series style temporal models (lines 463-504); AIMA contents.html: probabilistic reasoning over time, states and observations, finding the most likely sequence, and NLP language models (lines 361-382 and 621-660).
sentence_reviews.notes: The sources support language, sequences, observations, states, and temporal models. Biological sequences and trajectories are not directly supported by the inspected passages.

sentence_reviews.text: The role of Sequences, events & time series is to connect Discrete mathematics & logic, Signal processing & harmonic analysis to downstream capabilities including Waveforms, audio & spectrograms, Positional, temporal & coordinate encodings, Markov chains, MDPs, POMDPs & belief states.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: PML book1 long TOC: sequence models, 1d CNNs, transformers with positional encoding (lines 463-504); AIMA contents.html: probabilistic reasoning over time, HMMs, Kalman filters, MDPs, POMDPs, and Appendix B language/algorithm notes (lines 361-382, 435-457, and 776-780).
sentence_reviews.notes: Sequences, temporal models, positional encoding, and MDP/POMDP links are supported. Signal processing/harmonic analysis and audio/spectrograms are only indirectly supported.

sentence_reviews.evidence_location: PML book1 long TOC: Chapter 15 'Neural Networks for Sequences' and 1d CNNs for sequence classification/generation (lines 463-478).
sentence_reviews.notes: The evidence supports sequence modeling, but not waveforms, audio, or spectrograms explicitly.

sentence_reviews.text: substrate for Positional, temporal & coordinate encodings
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: PML book1 long TOC: transformer positional encoding section (lines 486-490) and sequence model sections (lines 463-478).
sentence_reviews.notes: Positional encoding is directly named in the sequence/transformer context; temporal and coordinate encodings are broader atlas synthesis.

sentence_reviews.text: substrate for Markov chains, MDPs, POMDPs & belief states
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA contents.html: probabilistic reasoning over time with HMMs and Markov-chain simulation, sequential decision problems, MDPs, POMDPs, and utilities over time (lines 345-382 and 435-457).
sentence_reviews.notes: The source directly supports temporal/sequential probabilistic and decision models. 'Belief states' are implied by POMDP and probabilistic filtering context rather than shown in the cited headings.

sentence_reviews.text: Encoding choices can discard information, amplify bias, or make important invariances difficult to learn. Its direct dependencies include Discrete mathematics & logic, Signal processing & harmonic analysis.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: PML book1 long TOC: preprocessing discrete input/text data and sequence models (lines 18-20 and 463-504); AIMA contents.html: Appendix B defining languages with BNF and chapter material on probabilistic reasoning over time (lines 776-780 and 361-382).
sentence_reviews.notes: Discrete representation and sequence modeling are supported, but the bias/invariance caution and signal-processing dependency are not directly supported by the inspected locations.

sentence_reviews.text: Sequences, events & time series rests on the formal or computational capabilities supplied by Discrete mathematics & logic.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA contents.html: Appendix B 'Defining Languages with Backus--Naur Form' and algorithms (lines 776-780); PML book1 long TOC: sequence models, language models, transformers, and token/text preprocessing (lines 18-20 and 463-504).
sentence_reviews.notes: Discrete language and sequence structures support the dependency; the exact rationale is synthesized.

sentence_reviews.text: Sequences, events & time series rests on the formal or computational capabilities supplied by Signal processing & harmonic analysis.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: PML book1 long TOC: 1d CNNs for sequence classification and generation, recurrent sequence models, and positional encoding (lines 463-490).
sentence_reviews.notes: The source supports neural sequence processing, but not signal processing or harmonic analysis as a formal prerequisite.

sentence_reviews.text: Sequences, events & time series rests on the formal or computational capabilities supplied by Algorithms & data structures.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA contents.html: Appendix B algorithms and pseudocode, Chapter 14 temporal-model inference algorithms, and Chapter 17 algorithms for MDPs (lines 776-780, 365-381, and 441-456).
sentence_reviews.notes: Temporal and sequence inference is presented algorithmically, but the source does not state this dependency verbatim.

edge_reviews.notes: Discrete language, token, and sequence structures make a requires/core relation defensible.
Signal processing is plausible for time series and waveforms, but inspected sources support neural sequence models more directly than signal-processing theory.
Sequence and temporal-model inference depend on algorithms; common strength is defensible because this edge is less semantically narrow than discrete math.
